title: Getting Started
summary: What Extend is and how to get going fast.

sections.heading: What Extend Is
sections.body: Simple, personal workout tracking. Log exercises, weights, sets, and reps. No ads, no subscriptions, no bloat. Everything is stored on your device.

sections.heading: What You Can Track
sections.body: Weights, kettlebells, barbells, bodyweight — any exercise you do. Build routines, log sessions, and watch your progress and personal records build over time.

sections.heading: Customization
sections.body: Make it yours. Rearrange the navigation bar, build a custom Dashboard with the stats you care about, choose light or dark theme, pick lbs or kg. The interface shows only what you choose.

sections.heading: Getting Started Fast
sections.body:
1. Add your exercises in the Exercises module.
2. Build a workout routine in Workouts.
3. Start the workout, log your sets, complete it.
4. Check Progress to see your history.

title: Settings
summary: Theme, units, navigation, dashboard, HealthKit, import/export, and reset.

sections.heading: Theme
sections.body: Light or Dark. Applies instantly. The Stick Figure Editor, Animation Studio, and Match Game always stay light regardless of this setting.

sections.heading: Weight Unit
sections.body: lbs or kg. Affects all weight display and entry throughout the app — logs, stat cards, personal records.

sections.heading: Keep Screen On
sections.body: Prevents the screen from locking during active timers, workouts, and voice trainer sessions. Uses slightly more battery.

sections.heading: Muscle Image Set
sections.body: Chooses the built-in anatomy images used in workout and muscle views — Option 1 (Male), Option 2 (Female), or None. Individual muscles can also have custom images overriding this.

sections.heading: Customize NavBar
sections.body:
Add, remove, and reorder modules across the top and bottom navigation bars. Each bar is configured independently. Tap arrows to reorder modules, toggle to hide or show them.

Color options: background color, optional gradient with a secondary color, and icon/text color. Changes apply instantly.

sections.heading: Customize Dashboard
sections.body:
Add, remove, resize, and reorder tiles on your Dashboard.

Tile types:
• Module Shortcut — opens a module.
• Stat Card — shows a live stat.
• Shortcut — launches a specific workout, timer, voice trainer, or exercise.
• Blank — launches a mini game or animation.

Stat cards available: Total Workouts, Day Streaks, Longest Streak, Total Time, Favorite Exercise, Favorite Day, Workout Frequency (14 days), Muscle Group Distribution (7 days), Volume This Week, Rest Days (14 days), Personal Record, 1RM Leaderboard.

Each tile supports: custom title, SF icon, size (small/medium/large), accent color and placement (none/left/top/right/bottom), and optional background tint.

sections.heading: Dashboard Header
sections.body: Optional banner at the top of the Dashboard. Toggle on or off. Customizable background color, gradient, custom image (from your photo library), and text color.

sections.heading: Export Workouts
sections.body: Exports your saved workout routines as a JSON file — useful as a backup or for transferring to another device. Exports routine definitions only, not logged session history.

sections.heading: Import Workouts
sections.body: Imports workout routines from a previously exported JSON file. Opens the system file picker. Imported workouts are added alongside existing ones.

sections.heading: Apple Health
sections.body:
Export: sends completed workout sessions to Apple Health as Strength Training activities.
Import: pulls workout activity data from Health into your Progress log.
Sync Now: manually trigger a sync. The app also syncs automatically on launch.

sections.heading: Reset App
sections.body: Deletes everything — logs, routines, exercises, muscles, equipment, timers, voice trainer configs, and all customizations. Returns the app to default. Cannot be undone. Requires confirmation.

title: Dashboard
summary: Home screen with customizable stat tiles and shortcuts.

sections.heading: Tiles
sections.body:
The Dashboard is a grid of tiles you build yourself. Each tile can be a stat card, a module shortcut, a quick-start shortcut, or a blank action tile.

Sizes: small (1 column), medium (2 columns), large (full row).

To edit: tap the gear icon on the Dashboard.

sections.heading: Stat Cards
sections.body:
• Total Workouts — lifetime count.
• Day Streaks — current consecutive days.
• Longest Streak — best streak ever.
• Total Time — total logged time.
• Favorite Exercise — most performed.
• Favorite Day — bar chart by day of week.
• Workout Frequency (14 days) — daily activity dots.
• Muscle Group Distribution — pie chart, last 7 days.
• Volume This Week — bar chart, last 7 weeks.
• Rest Days (14 days) — non-workout days in past two weeks.
• Personal Record — recent PRs per exercise.
• 1RM Leaderboard — top estimated one-rep maxes.

sections.heading: Shortcuts
sections.body: A Shortcut tile launches a specific saved workout, timer, voice trainer config, or exercise directly — no navigating through a list.

title: Workouts
summary: Build routines and log training sessions.

sections.heading: Creating a Workout
sections.body: Tap +. Name it, then add exercises from your library. Group exercises together to form complexes — each group gets a color. Grouped exercises are performed back to back.

sections.heading: Logging a Session
sections.body: Tap a workout to expand it, then tap the play button to start. Log each set by entering weight and reps, then tap the checkmark. Previous session values are shown as reference. Tap 'Complete Workout' when done.

sections.heading: Personal Records and 1RM
sections.body: The app tracks PRs and estimated one-rep maxes automatically as you log sets. View them on the Dashboard (stat cards) or per-exercise in the Exercises module.

sections.heading: Anatomy View
sections.body: Tap the ⓘ button next to an exercise during a session to see which muscle groups it targets.

sections.heading: History
sections.body: Completed sessions appear in Progress. To see history for a specific workout, tap the chart icon on its row.

title: Generate
summary: Auto-generate a workout from your equipment and muscle filters.

sections.heading: How It Works
sections.body: Choose which equipment and muscle groups to include, then tap Generate. The app picks a random set of matching exercises and builds a workout for you.

sections.heading: Saving
sections.body: Tap Save, give it a name, and it appears in your Workouts list.

sections.heading: Filter Presets
sections.body: Tap the sliders icon to save your current filter as a named preset. Switch between presets (e.g. 'Home' vs. 'Gym') with one tap.

title: Quick Workout
summary: Log a single exercise session without building a full routine.

sections.heading: How It Works
sections.body: Tap any exercise to immediately start a session for that one movement. Log sets, reps, and weight, then complete it. No routine setup needed.

sections.heading: Favorites
sections.body: Star exercises to pin them as tiles at the top for instant one-tap access.

title: Timer
summary: Create and run interval timers for any training style.

sections.heading: Creating a Timer
sections.body: Tap +. Set a name, work duration, rest duration, and round count. Optional: warmup, cooldown, sound and vibration alerts.

sections.heading: Running
sections.body: Tap a timer in the list to open it, then tap Start. The screen shows current phase (work/rest), time remaining, and round. Tap Pause to pause, Resume to continue.

sections.heading: Favorites and History
sections.body: Star a timer for the Favorites grid. Tap the clock icon for usage history. Tap the chart icon for stats.

title: Progress
summary: Your full workout history — calendar and timeline views, plus journal.

sections.heading: Calendar View
sections.body: Days with logged workouts are marked. Tap a date to see what was logged. Tap the month header to change months.

sections.heading: Timeline View
sections.body: Sessions as cards in reverse chronological order. Filter by week or month. Search by workout name or notes.

sections.heading: Activity Ribbon
sections.body: Toggle the ribbon on to see a 60-day heat map of training frequency at a glance.

sections.heading: Journal
sections.body: Tap the compose button to write a free-form note. Journal entries appear in the timeline alongside workout logs.

title: Exercises
summary: Your exercise library — create, organize, and track stats per movement.

sections.heading: Adding an Exercise
sections.body: Tap +. Enter a name, assign muscle groups and equipment (optional), and add a custom image if you want.

sections.heading: Editing and Deleting
sections.body: Swipe left to edit or delete. Deleting removes the exercise from any workouts that include it.

sections.heading: Favorites
sections.body: Star an exercise to pin it to the Favorites grid and make it available in Quick Workout.

sections.heading: Stats and History
sections.body: Tap the chart icon for stats (total sets, average weight, PRs). Tap the clock icon for full session history for that exercise.

title: Muscles
summary: Define muscle groups used in exercise tracking and anatomy views.

sections.heading: Adding Muscle Groups
sections.body: Tap +. Name the muscle group. It becomes available to assign to exercises, which powers anatomy diagrams and distribution stats.

sections.heading: Custom Images
sections.body: Each muscle group can have a custom photo overriding the built-in image set. Tap the muscle, then tap the image to replace it.

sections.heading: Stats
sections.body: Tap the chart icon to see training frequency and which exercises target that muscle.

title: Equipment
summary: Add equipment to categorize exercises and filter generated workouts.

sections.heading: Adding Equipment
sections.body: Tap +. Name it (e.g. Barbell, Dumbbell, Resistance Band). Assign equipment to exercises in the Exercise editor.

sections.heading: Filtering
sections.body: In Generate, enable the Equipment filter to only include exercises matching your available gear.

sections.heading: Stats
sections.body: Tap the chart icon to see which exercises use a piece of equipment and how often it shows up in your logs.

title: Trainer
summary: Voice-guided sessions — the app calls out your cues at timed intervals.

sections.heading: How It Works
sections.body: Write cues (one per line), set your timing, and the app reads them aloud during your session. Works great for boxing, martial arts, circuits, or any interval training.

sections.heading: Creating a Config
sections.body:
Tap +. Name it, enter cues (e.g. 'Jab', '1-2', 'Right Hook'), then set:
• Rounds and round length
• Rest between rounds
• Delay between spoken cues
• Start warning countdown
• Rest end warning
• Cooldown period

sections.heading: Random Order
sections.body: Shuffles cues each round so sessions stay unpredictable.

sections.heading: Running a Session
sections.body: Tap a config to start. The screen shows the current cue, round, and elapsed time. Tap Pause to pause. Tap X or 'Complete Session' early — both ask for confirmation. Completing saves the session to Progress.

sections.heading: Favorites
sections.body: Star a config to pin it to the Favorites grid for one-tap access.

title: Stick Figure Animator
summary: Pose a stick figure frame by frame and export as an animated GIF.

sections.heading: Frame Editor
sections.body: Drag joints to pose the figure. Use X/Y controls to position it on the canvas. Adjust scale, limb thickness, and body proportions with the sliders below.

sections.heading: Saving Frames
sections.body: Tap 'Save Frame', give it a name. Open the frame list to load, copy, rename, or delete frames.

sections.heading: Animation Studio
sections.body: Tap 'Open Studio' to arrange frames into a sequence, set speed, preview, and export as a GIF.

sections.heading: Adding Objects
sections.body: Tap 'Add Object' to place emojis, images, or colored boxes on the canvas. Each object can be moved, scaled, and rotated independently.

sections.heading: Playing
sections.body: Swap adjacent tiles to match 3 or more of the same type. Larger matches earn powerups. Work through 500 levels.

sections.heading: Progress
sections.body: Your level saves automatically. Add a Match tile to the Dashboard to see your current level at a glance.
